checks.finding: REQ-012 requires feature mode to fail when any required full-profile artifact is absent, while AC-009 refers only to 'all seven full-profile Phase 1 outputs' without enumerating them; REQ-009 separately requires a traceability template, leaving it unclear whether traceability.md is part of the checked artifact set. REQ-016 also requires nonblank Layer Spec references for every 'applicable' requirement without defining applicability or its representation. A checker or task-review implementer therefore cannot deterministically choose the required file list or decide which traceability rows may omit a layer reference. These are product-level workflow contracts that must be fixed before design and task decomposition, not implementation details. Major is justified because reasonable implementations can enforce different artifact and traceability sets and thus produce incompatible pass/fail behavior.

The requirements, acceptance criteria, non-goals, and behavioral contracts consistently preserve explicit feature-mode compatibility, LITE exclusion, existing review stages, and the Draft-to-Approved boundary; no direct conflict makes the requested behavior impossible.

The acceptance tests and behavioral contracts cover absent and complete feature artifacts, invalid and traversal-like slugs, explicit empty/uppercase/underscore selectors, LITE exclusion, existing-file preservation, unaffected bugfix/refactor layers, absent visual input, legacy repository-only behavior, review-input tampering, Draft rejection, and authorized approval.

The Open Questions section is explicitly empty, and Validated Baseline And Decisions resolves diagram format, review-stage extension, current-version baseline, and the selected 1.4.0 release; remaining risks have acceptance surfaces.

REQ-017 and AC-016 make the governance boundary observable: task review leaves tasks Draft, implement-task rejects Draft tasks, and only a human edit or active signed sdd-sudo transition can approve selected tasks.

REQ-012/AC-009 do not enumerate the seven artifacts that feature validation must require, and REQ-016/AC-008 do not define how layer applicability is determined. Consequently implementation-policy design and task decomposition would have to invent product behavior for the checker inventory and traceability enforcement. This belongs to specification review because it determines externally observable validation outcomes. Major is justified because the ambiguity blocks deterministic downstream contracts but does not create an unsafe approval-boundary conflict.
